desc:
The road keeps going, leading to uncommon places. If you follow it westwards, it
will lead you to the gates of the Grey Havens, just at the shore of the sea.
South of the road, you see a very rough field and to the north, a grove so dense
that it is impossible to walk through it.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road bends here, going south and west. Towards east and north, you can see
very dense groves which makes passage in those directions extremely difficult.
However, you can barely manage to get into the grove through a small passage in
the north. To your south, you see empty lands, too uneven to allow passage.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road bends here, going north and east. To the east, you can see a field of
tall, green grass. In the northeast, you see a very dense group of tall and dark
trees standing silently. You get a strange, uneasy feeling just looking at those
trees. To your west are some rough looking fields which grow too uneven in your
south to pass through.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
You are now standing in a rough field full of stones and thick grass that makes
it very difficult to walk on it. To the north and the east, you can see the road
leading to the Grey Havens. To your south, is a thick grove with large, tall
trees. However, the path there is full of jagged rocks which make it impossible
to reach that grove.
name: Rough Field

desc:
The grove gets very dense here but it's not very hard to walk through it. All
around you, you can see a large number of trees with fat trunks that look so
hard that you can imagine why no one ever bothers to cut wood out of them. It is
strange, you reflect, how old things sometimes hide still older ones.
name: Dense Grove

desc:
Around you, the trees seem to open up as if you were walking by a path, even
though the ground is rough and the passage is not easy. You can see the road in
the south through the branches. The grove gets a lot denser in the east and the
north, thereby preventing you from going in those directions.
name: Dense Grove

desc:
Hidden from prying eyes, you have stumbled upon a small Elven village nestled
among the trees here. The main street runs east-west here and faint voices can
be heard off to your left. You can barely pick out the Elven houses as they
blend in with the forest, but here and there you catch a glimpse of a twinkling
eye and a laughing face looking back at you. You can see buildings both to the
east and to the west.
name: Elven Village

desc:
As you walk on the main street in this village, you feel at peace and happy.
Around you, you can pick out many houses, big and small, among the trees. To the
north, you see a heavy wooden door with strange symbols drawn on it that seem
mysterious. From under the door, you detect a faint blue light which moves your
heart. To the south is a magnificent temple dedicated to the Valar. The people
here like to lead simple and happy lives, it appears but are strong enough to
withstand attacks on their lives or lifestyles.
name: Elven Village

desc:
The place is calm and silent. This grand temple is dedicated to the All-Mighty
Valar, Lords of Arda, and whose memory is almost lost for the common elf. An
altar of fine marble can be seen, and in the walls ancient paintings revive for
the observer forgotten ages.
name: Temple to the Valar

desc:
This path hidden in the grove is not used very often, as you can see. All around
is silent, and the atmosphere is undisturbed by any sound of activity except
your steps and one occasional bird singing somewhere in the trees. To the north,
you think you see a village, but it is hard to say since it seems to be as quiet
as everything in this place.
name: Hidden Path

desc:
Steel doors and hard-wood furniture is what you see around you. The floor is
hard and smooth. Some items of metal armour are hung on the walls which look
well polished and strong. The size of the armour amazes you when you realise
that men who wear these items are bound to be extra-ordinarily big and strong.
To the north, you can see what looks like a place to eat for the guards. To the
west, you can see beds implying a resting area for them.
name: Guards' Room

desc:
This is the village guards' mess hall. Long oaken tables polished and stained to
a lustrous brown sheen occupy the centre of the room; benches of similar
construction stand next to them. A savoury smell wafts into the room from the
east.
name: Guards' Mess

desc:
This place is spacious and very tidy. You can walk in the broad aisles between
the long kitchen counters. The stoves are all arranged in the centre of the room
directly under the chimney. Shelves on the walls are all stacked with flour,
spices and other kitchen material. The kitchen implements are arranged on the
walls on hooks. A window opens to the north where you see a dark grove behind a
small clear piece of land.
name: Kitchen

desc:
This is a small room artfully decorated and furnished to make it appear bigger.
Tapestries, ancient beyond reckoning, line the walls. Many heroic figures stare
out at you as if watching you and judging your worth. From the east comes a
small steady glow, too steady to be a fire. The glow seems to be radiating
goodness which blesses all that is good and instils horror in the evil hearts.
name: A Wooden House

desc:
This room is rather small, but the lighting gives off the illusion of
magnificence. Tapestries adorn all four walls, as in the outer room. Vistas of
vast lands long forgotten seem to draw the viewer into their splendour and hold
them there. The entire decor is as a shrine to an age long past, with wondrous
things that Arda will probably never witness again. The bright glow which
provides a light as brilliant and beautiful as the distant stars comes from a
small phial embedded in a stone in the centre of the room. The light is so
brilliant and encompassing it casts no shadow, and the light enhances the effect
of the tapestries. A feeling of importance emanates from this phial, and its
removal from this room would most likely bring down the wrath of the elves on
the thief.
name: The Refuge

desc:
Birds are chirping sweet melodies and small furry animals rush past you as they
search for food among the dense trees to the north. A small grassy park has been
fenced in for the little children to play in. To the south, you see a simple
structure which houses the priest to the Valar. Far towards east, you observe
that a small area has been cleared among the forest for growing crops.
name: Elven Village

desc:
The road is perfectly straight here heading west or east. On both sides of the
road, you can see a very dense group of tall, dark trees that prohibit you from
passing through them. Looking at the trees gives you an uneasy feeling and you
just want to get to a safe place somewhere. Oddly enough, you can feel something
good somewhere close in the west.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road goes east and west without a care in the world for what exists on
either side of it. As you walk, the trees far in the north appear to take on
strange forms, which amuses you. Heading westwards will take you to the gates of
Grey Havens near the sea. In the north, near the road, you can see a small hut
which appears abandoned. Towards south, you see some quiet grassy fields.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road bends here going south and west. In the east, you can see some fields
of tall, green grass swaying slightly in the breeze. The passage north is too
densely populated with tall, dark trees to allow you to pass through. The road
is in a pretty good condition and it feels pleasant to travel on it.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road flows north and east from here, cutting through the quiet, grassy
fields. Beyond the grass, far in the south, you can see a dark grove of trees
that may be infested with wild animals. Far in the north, you see a similar
growth of trees. The road, however, provides a strange sense of security and you
feel glad for some strange reason.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road goes east and west here, leading to the Grey Havens in the west. You
see fields of tall, lush green grass on either side of the road and gloomy
looking groves of tall trees further beyond the grass. An oak tree stands on the
north bank of the road providing shelter to weary travellers.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road seems endless and the lands around it just too quiet. You can see
fields of green grass in either direction, all standing in complete silence.
However, the road provides comfort and a feeling of safety. Remembering your
destination, you again start moving fast towards it. Perhaps someday you will
come back and find more about the land.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road flows like a river going east and west. Fields of tall and lush green
grass are on either side of the road. Dark groves, possibly inhabited by wild
animals, grow beyond the grass. The fields near the road are very quiet except
for noises made by some insects. However, just being on the road makes you feel
safe and secure.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road goes on and on, taking the adventuring soul to lands both strange and
very seductive. On each side of the road, you see a vast stretch of grasslands
swaying in the wind. Beyond the grass, in either direction you observe dark
groves which might be inhabited with wild animals. To the west somewhere, lies
the sea and the city of Cirdan, called Grey Havens.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
Here the road makes a turn from south to west, but a small and well-used trail
leads to the north across some fairly bleak looking plains. To the Far Downs
rise a few leagues and then cut off abruptly. A cut in the slope is easily
noticed east of a small settlement not far away to the southeast.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road bends east-north here and enters the Far Downs. To the north and south
the downs rise gently while the road climbs to the east towards a cut in the
hills.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road here runs straight east-west through the Far Downs. A couple of miles
to the east lies the small settlement of Greenholm where one may rest one's
weary bones. The landscape all about looks fairly grim.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
Just to the south of the road to the Grey Havens is a path which leads to the
small village of Greenholm, where many a weary traveller has spent the night.
There appear to be a few buildings to the east of the village and an inn to the
west. You can see some thick smoke coming from the chimney of the inn. The
village is surrounded by the far downs, which stretch as far as the eye can see.
name: Greenholm Junction

desc:
South of Greenholm the land is criss-crossed by dry-stone walls separating rough
fields from even rougher downs. The land gently rises to the south and east it
seems to cut off in a line from north to south - the edge of the scarp it would
seem. To the north a small track leads through some fields in which the odd
horse stands eating grass. The track runs down to Greenholm and the back of the
courtyard to an Inn.
name: Far Downs

desc:
Here the road slices through the chalk escarpment, to the north and south dull
white walls rise up forcing you east or west. The road climbs up through the cut
towards the hamlet of Greenholm, somewhere to rest your weary bones?
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road runs east-west through the Far Downs. To the east the downs rise and
fall gently but to the west the landscape is dominated by a sheer chalk wall
rearing up out of the grasses. The road climbs through a notch in the scarp,
angling upwards toward the hamlet of Greenholm.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road continues east-west through the Far Downs on its long journey from the
Grey Havens to Imladris. The land around is fairly safe but somewhat bleak in
nature.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road continues east to west through the Far Downs on its long journey from
Imladris to the Grey Havens. The land around is fairly safe but somewhat bleak
in nature.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road bends west-north here, winding its way through the Far Downs. A few
leagues further to the west you can see a rather prominent chalk cliff through
which the road runs.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
Here the road takes an east-south bend as it winds its way through the gently
rolling hills of the Far Downs. To the west is what appears to be a pass of
sorts and to the north a trail seems to climb up through the downs.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The East Road runs east-west through the Far Downs. There is little in the way
of habitation out here and little road traffic these days either.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
You trudge onwards along the ancient road, deeper into the silent, brooding
slopes and vales of the Far Downs. All around the hills stand quiet but for the
blowing of the wind over the grasses. The road continues ever on.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
You walk upon a venerable road. Stretching amongst rolling hills, leading you
ever closer to the sea, and the home of the Shipwright. Your boots break the
silence on gravel polished by endless generations of fellow travellers, and you
breathe in the clear air, blown by breezes out of the downs. All is quiet here,
though you cannot be sure if the silence is serene or threatening.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
This place gives you an idea about the lifestyle of the priest. The room is
barely furnished other than a few chairs and a table for occasions when the
people need the priest's advice, perhaps. A small lamp in a niche in the west
wall is the only light source. The floor is covered with a thin carpet which
feels quite soft to the feet.
name: Priest's Abode

desc:
This area has been cut out of the dark forest that surrounds it for crops. It
spreads about half a mile in each direction. When you look eastwards, you see
what appears to be a large building right next to the forest boundary. To the
west is the elven village. You are walking on a small muddy path that goes
directly towards the structure in the east.
name: Quiet Fields

desc:
This building serves as a refuge if you get caught in the rain while out in the
fields, but its main purpose is to store the surplus food grains for use later.
You can see storerooms sectioned off to hold different grains, but you are not
able to access those piles. You also see large haystacks and piles of oat near
the walls.
name: Large Barn

desc:
You are in a large room which is filled with cereals and fodder for the farm
animals. The roof looks sturdy and leak-proof. The barn is to the north and you
can see the fields in the west through a small window. The walls of this room
are thick and strong, and there are some strange weeds thrown about the room,
probably to keep out the pests.
name: Storage Chamber

desc:
This room is dark and quiet and the shadows loom large. The walls are dry and
hard. A layer of dust has settled on the floor, indicating that this place has
not been used in a long time. Thick stone columns support the roof every twenty
feet or so.
name: Empty Corridor

desc:
You find yourself in a damp room with a low ceiling. The walls of solid but bare
stone return an echo of every sound you make. The floor is covered with dust and
mud, and there is fungus growing on the walls. As you look up, you see that the
roof has cracks in it that allow water to seep in. You can sense something
strange but no matter how hard you try, you cannot figure it out.
name: Abandoned Hut

desc:
You are standing in a field just north of the road that leads to and away from
the Grey Havens. The field is covered with soft, green grass. All is quiet
around here but you can hear high-pitched noises made by some insects that you
cannot see or locate. This would be a good place to camp if you wanted to stay a
while - since there is nothing here to disturb you.
name: Quiet Fields

desc:
The road is just to the south of you. Soft, lush green grass covers the whole
area. The sounds made by wind, as it passes over the grass, make you yearn for
your home and your friends. Perhaps you should stay here a little while and let
that feeling take its course. But if you have to ride on, you might just let the
memory of this place take a hold in your mind.
name: Quiet Fields

desc:
The grass has grown much taller here, but it is still soft and green. This
grassy field ends a little distance north from here, where a dense and gloomy
looking grove begins. The branches of the trees in the grove are so inter-
twined as to make it impossible to go in there. Besides, you have the distinct
impression that going in there might not turn out to be very safe.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
You have entered the home of a family of wolves. Perhaps it would be wise to
retreat if you can. The walls of this place have scratch marks all over them. On
the ground, you can see a number of tracks of some big animal. A few bones are
lying around here and there. As soon as you entered, something began to move
fast, and then they came into view as they made a lunge towards you.
name: Lair of the Wolves

desc:
The grove is dense and almost impossible to walk through, here. Everywhere you
look, the trees loom large over you, reducing your long range view. Now and
then, everything becomes very quiet. Then, strange sounds come from all around
you and a nasty feeling makes your spine tingle. Unless you are a skilled
hunter, you wish to be out of here soon.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
The grove is much less dense here and you can see a good part of the sky. In the
north and west, the grove becomes too thick to allow entry. All around you, the
ground is covered with thick bushes with sharp thorns. The sounds that you hear
here, make you want to leave this place quickly and return to safety while you
can.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
You are standing in a very dense grove of tall, dark trees. The branches are so
much intertwined that travelling between the trees is burdensome. You find
however, that this place is not as quiet as it appeared from outside. Dim but
sharp sounds of activity can be heard coming from every direction. Something
dangerous and wild lives here, it appears.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
The grove seems to be somehow lighter here, as the trees open up. The way in
each direction is covered with thick bushes and thorns, promising a rough
journey. Loud and wild noises are coming from all around you and this makes you
want to leave this place quickly. Something unseen is definitely moving around
out there.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
You are surrounded by tall trees in every direction. This grove is a very
strange one. One moment everything is quiet and still, and the next second,
everything seems to come alive. There is no evil here, you feel, but this is a
region inhabited by those creations of the gods that are a little wild.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
There is a large pond here with water that looks drinkable. Near the pond's
edge, you can see a huge number of tracks made by animals both big and small.
Occasionally, you may find an animal resting here after a hard days work. In the
south, you see the grove but in all other directions, the branches are so
heavily intertwined that you cannot even move a yard into the trees there.
name: Quiet Pond

desc:
The grove gets a little dense here. You don't detect any evil presence but this
is no place for a good-hearted adventurer, either. The constant noise of animals
big and small, moving around makes you very uncomfortable. But, you realise that
this would be a good place to try your hunting skills.
name: Dark Grove

desc:
The grove seems to be endless. In every direction, the trees are growing so
dense that it is hard to make your way through them. The grove gets silent
sometimes and then, a wolf rushes by after a rabbit. You try your best to hide
from all the wild animals here, but they say that the wild ones hunt by their
noses as much as by their eyes and ears.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
This grove is incredibly dense here, and this makes every move onerous. All
around you is dark and gloomy. The wind sometimes makes the branches move and
produce creaking noises that are eerie. Wild animals are bound to be around.
Once in a while, you hear a loud roar or a howl, and your blood freezes for a
moment.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
A small number of trees grow along with the grass here. The presence of thick
bushes and plants makes it a little harder to walk. To the south and west, you
can see open grassy fields. Towards north, the vegetation grows so thick as to
deny you access into it. An occasional animal comes in sight now and then, but
mostly you don't see much animal life. Insects abound here, though.
name: Dense Vegetation

desc:
You are in a field where the grass reaches up to your waist. The ground is firm
and you have no trouble walking through the grass. This field ends very suddenly
in the north. A very dense and dark grove with rather tall trees is visible as
you look north. Upon careful observation, you notice a small gap between the
trees which allows you to enter the grove if you so desire. Far to the south,
you can see the road leading to the Grey Havens. The fields continue in the east
and the west.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The grassy fields are very silent. Any movement or noise is merely the wind
rustling the grass as it passes over the fields. The whole place appears to be
safe and peaceful. The grass grows taller in the north and in the east. To the
west, the fields continue.
name: Quiet Fields

desc:
The lush green grass rises up to your knees here. But the ground is firm and so
you can walk here without too much trouble. The fields continue to the west,
where the grass grows less high, and to the east. In the north you can see the
vegetation growth become quite dense. The road to Grey Havens is to your south.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The vegetation grows thick here, with tall and dark trees beginning to dominate
the landscape. Towards the east, you see some grassy plains and in the west,
there is a dark grove of trees which looks dangerous. There is a silence in
these woods that is both eerie and soothing at the same time. In the south, you
see large fields covered with lush green grass.
name: Light Woods

desc:
A large variety of plants and undergrowth can be found here. The ground is quite
soft and the vegetation is quite dense. In the west, you can see a dark grove of
tall, dark trees, which appears quite frightening. In the south, you see some
more trees. As you look east, you see vast, grassy fields covered with soft,
green grass. To the north a single tree can be seen in the distance.
name: Light Woods

desc:
A solitary tree can be seen in this small clearing among the woods. Its branches
seem particularly robust and able to support a large amount of weight. Wooden
beams have been attached to the trunk, allowing one to climb upwards.
name: An Ancient Tree

desc:
Several wooden planks and walls have been erected to form a small house within
this large tree. Branches and leaves cover you overhead, forming a natural
barrier against the rain and weather, while letting the sunlight beam in during
the day. To the east, a door leads out onto a rather thick branch.
name: In a Tree House

desc:
The wind whips across these plains with very little in the way of protection. If
solitude is what you require then this is the right place to have come. The only
sign of life is a trail, obviously regularly used, amongst the numerous animal
tracks. To the east rise the Far Downs and far to the west, on a clear day, can
be seen the Tower Hills beyond which lies Mithlond.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
This is the edge of the Far Downs. North and west lie nothing but bleak, open
plains dotted with the odd scrub bush. Far to the west, on a clear day, it is
possible to make out the Tower Hills (S. Emyn Beraid). The ground here rises and
falls quite gently, always climbing to the east though. Animal trails
criss-cross the area, obviously there is a fair amount of wildlife in these
parts, even if there is little in the way of civilisation in evidence. A small
pile of stones has been piled here.
name: Edge of the Far Downs

desc:
The wind whips across these plains with very little in the way of protection. If
solitude is what you require then this is the right place to have come. The only
sign of life is a trail, obviously regularly used, amongst the numerous animal
tracks. To the east rise the Far Downs and far to the west, on a clear day, can
be seen the Tower Hills beyond which lies Mithlond. To the north lie barren,
windswept plains stretching all the way to the estates of the once powerful
ruling families of Arthedain, which spread over the Emyn Uial and along the
shores of Lake Evendim. A small pile of stones has been set here.
name: Bleak Plains

desc:
The scarp face here drops in height and finally plunges below the ground. Travel
eastwards is now quite possible... to the north the land rolls over barren
plains towards the Emyn Uial and the lands of northwestern Arthedain. The wind
whips ferociously around you, tugging at your clothing. Animal tracks zig-zag
about through the grass and heather. A fine place for solitude and being at one
with the elements... A small pile of stones has been set here.
name: End of the Scarp

desc:
You stand here on the northern edge of the Far Downs, the land rolling north
over somewhat dismal and bleak looking grasslands and heath. Far to the north
lie the Emyn Uial by Lake Evendim, home to some of the estates of the most
powerful families of Arnor of old. The landscape looks bleak and forbidding and
travel in that direction is pushed from your mind. To the south the downs rise
and fall in austere splendour. A small pile of stones has been set here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
You stand here on the northern edge of the Far Downs, the land rolling north
over somewhat dismal and bleak looking grasslands and heath. Far to the north
lie the Emyn Uial by Lake Evendim, home to some of the estates of the most
powerful families of Arnor of old. The landscape looks bleak and forbidding and
travel in that direction is pushed from your mind. To the south the downs rise
and fall in austere splendour.
name: Far Downs

desc:
You stand here on the northern edge of the Far Downs, the land rolling north
over somewhat dismal and bleak looking grasslands and heath. Far to the north
lie the Emyn Uial by Lake Evendim, home to some of the estates of the most
powerful families of Arnor of old. The landscape looks bleak and forbidding and
travel in that direction is pushed from your mind. To the south the downs rise
and fall in austere splendour. The valley continues eastwards toward a rather
inhospitable looking vale. You can see quite clearly that this place is not
visited by wildlife on a regular basis. In the distance a ruin is discernible.
name: Bleak Vale

desc:
You are standing in what is left of a once great tower (or so it would seem)
dating possibly from the First Age! The wind can hardly be heard down here and
all seems very eerie. Numerous mosses and lichens cover the walls and stones of
the place, the odd stunted bush hugging the remaining standing walls. It would
appear that someone was here not too long ago... there is what looks like the
remains of a fire and a crude shelter, which has collapsed into a heap of
branches and matted grass.
name: Ruined Tower

desc:
Standing amongst the hills you experience a strange feeling as you look to the
west. The wind doesn't seem to blow quite so strongly, or loudly, here and no
animal tracks lead down into the vale to the west at all... strange? North and
east the downs seem to roll away, clouds scudding along on the perpetual
winds... Now what does lie west!?
name: Far Downs

desc:
Standing amongst the hills you experience a strange feeling as you look to the
west. The wind doesn't seem to blow quite so strongly, or loudly, here and no
animal tracks lead down into the vale to the west at all... strange? North and
east the downs seem to roll away, clouds scudding along on the perpetual
winds... Now what does lie west!?
name: Far Downs

desc:
You stand at the bottom of a vale so quiet and still that it seems to have a
presence all of its own. The grass here is short and pale, as if it can only
just get a foothold with which to grow. A few rivulets of water trickle over the
moss covered stones, one of which seems to have had something carved upon it. To
the north lies the remnants of an ancient tower. This really does not seem like
a good place to be hanging around in...
name: An Eerie Vale

desc:
Standing amongst the hills you experience a strange feeling as you look to the
east. The wind doesn't seem to blow quite so strongly, or loudly, here and no
animal tracks lead down into the vale to the east at all... strange? North and
west the downs seem to roll away, clouds scudding along on the perpetual
winds... Now what does lie east!?
name: Far Downs

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals. To the south is a small stand of trees. A small pile of
stones has been set here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
You are at the foot of the chalk scarp face. The stained whitish wall rears up
to the west making travel that way impossible. The trail you have been following
has brought you to a junction, with a small, well-tended path leading off into
the small wooded area to the south. The wind blows hard here seemingly all the
time - maybe a quirk in the lie of the land... whatever the case, there is a hut
visible in the woods which could provide a certain amount of shelter should you
so desire it. A large pile of stones has been built here.
name: A Dip in the Far Downs

desc:
Standing on the edge of the scarp face you can see the downs rolling east
towards the White Hills and further on to the Shire. The gentle dip and climb
speckled by odd pools dotted here and there occasionally reflecting the light.
The view west is of a gentle downslope unfolding into plains as bleak as the Far
Downs and thence to the Emyn Beraid a few leagues further. Unless you can fly it
looks like any easterly travel will be out of the question due to the sheer
drop.
name: Far Downs Scarp Edge

desc:
Standing on the edge of the scarp face you can see the downs rolling east
towards the White Hills and further on to the Shire. The gentle dip and climb
speckled by odd pools dotted here and there occasionally reflecting the light.
The view west is of a gentle downslope unfolding into plains as bleak as the Far
Downs and thence to the Emyn Beraid a few leagues further. Unless you can fly it
looks like any easterly travel will be out of the question due to the sheer
drop.
name: Far Downs Scarp Edge

desc:
Standing on the edge of the scarp face you can see the downs rolling east
towards the White Hills and further on to the Shire. The gentle dip and climb
speckled by odd pools dotted here and there occasionally reflecting the light.
The view west is of a gentle downslope unfolding into plains as bleak as the Far
Downs and thence to the Emyn Beraid a few leagues further. Unless you can fly it
looks like any easterly travel will be out of the question due to the sheer
drop. To the south the East Road cuts its way through a break in the scarp.
name: Far Downs Scarp Edge

desc:
Standing on the edge of the scarp face you can see the downs rolling east
towards the White Hills and further on to the Shire. The gentle dip and climb

name: Far Downs Scarp Edge

desc:
Standing on the edge of the scarp face you can see the downs rolling east
towards the White Hills and further on to the Shire. The gentle dip and climb

The view west is of a gentle downslope unfolding into plains as bleak as the Far
Downs and thence to the Emyn Beraid a few leagues further. Unless you can fly it
looks like any easterly travel will be out of the question due to the sheer
drop. A deep gully blocks all further southerly passage.
name: Far Downs Scarp Edge

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals. To the south the downs appear to be ending, giving way to
bleak, unwelcoming plains.
name: Far Downs

desc:
Once off the road the terrain becomes quite rough: long grasses blow in the
wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any
form of habitation in the area - the road to the northwest is the only sign that
anybody ever comes near here.
name: Far Downs

desc:

wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any

name: Far Downs

form of habitation in the area - the road to the north being the only sign that
anybody ever comes near here.
name: Far Downs

name: Far Downs

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak and austere view to travellers. The only
sounds to be heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional
cry of a falcon riding the air. Numerous animal tracks crisscross the area at
regular intervals. To the south the downs seem to end, giving way to even
bleaker plains.
name: A Dip in the Far Downs

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals. To the south the downs appear to be ending, giving way to
bleak, unwelcoming plains.
name: Far Downs

desc:
It is a lot quieter and far less smokey at this end of the room, away from the
bar and the fire. A wide counter stands just in front of the staircase that
climbs its way to the upper level of the inn. A large sign hangs from the
counter. There are some paintings of famous hobbits hanging on the walls.
name: Inn of the Seven Stars

desc:
Quite obviously, this warm building is the Inn of the Seven Stars. Many hobbits
and weary travellers frequently drink themselves into oblivion within its lively
walls. There is a huge bar on the far wall. Many kegs of fine ale run along the
length of the shelf behind the bar. At one end of the bar is a huge fireplace,
where a roaring fire heats the entire room. In the centre of the room and to the
north are many tables and benches. A large staircase can just about be seen
through the smoke coming from the hobbit pipes and the fire.
name: The Seven Stars Inn

desc:
The poor decoration of this house indicates it must be the home of a rather poor
hobbit. There is a small bed in the corner and a rickety looking table and
chairs in the centre of the room. A small window in the west wall looks over the
downs.
name: A Small Hobbit Home

desc:
A small path leads from the road just north of here to the tiny village of
Greenholm. It appears to be mainly used by the travellers on their way to either
the Shire or the Grey Havens. The typically cautious hobbits who live in
Greenholm to the south have probably rarely left their pleasant village. To the
north is the road to the Grey Havens.
name: Path to Greenholm

desc:
You are in an empty room which was probably occupied by some poor hobbit family.
It looks like this small building wasn't abandoned very long time ago, because
there is only a thin dust layer on the floor and you can barely discern spider
webs in the corners.
name: The Abandoned Building

desc:
By hobbit standards, this is quite a plush living room of a house. You can see a
small bedroom joining the room and also a small kitchen. The house was obviously
not designed for many hobbits to live in, it might have been a first house for a
pair of newlywed hobbits.
name: A Small House in Greenholm

desc:
Many hobbit houses surround the eastern end of Green Street. To the south is a
very expensive looking house which must be the home of some important hobbit. It
has been recently painted and looks very pretty with its flower garden around
it. The houses to the north and east are less remarkable. Although they have
gardens, none of them are quite as good as the one to the south.
name: Green Street

desc:
The main street of the small village of Greenholm runs from east to west here.
Many hobbits can be seen going about their daily business. To the south is a
small shop, with a large sign over the doorway. The main street continues the
east and to the west. To the north a path leads up a small rise towards the road
to the Grey Havens.
name: Green Street

desc:
The main street of Greenholm ends at the Inn of the Seven Stars. There is a
small pictured sign hanging from a tall post just outside the door. The inn
looks warm and cosy and the smoke pouring out of the chimney indicates that
there is a hot fire burning fiercely inside. Attached to the Inn just south of
here is a small stable. There is a small hobbit house just to the north.
name: Green Street

desc:
The shop is a surprisingly busy shop for a small village in the middle of the
Far Downs. From the looks of the shelves, it sells nearly everything a hobbit
might need for every day life. The shop was named in the memory of that
legendary hobbit gardener Holman the Greenhanded whose descendants formed the
Greenhand clan and settled this village many years ago.
name: Holman's Supply Shop

desc:
Someone must have taken a lot of effort to make this splendid living room look
so wonderful. There are two comfortable armchairs in front of the fireplace and
pleasant tapestries adorn all the walls. There is a showcase full of expensive
looking pipes standing next to the stairs which lead up to the upper floor of
the house. A thick rug covers most of the floor of this room.
name: Living Room in the Greenhands

desc:
South of the road the terrain becomes quite rough: long grasses blow in the
wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any
real travel through the area despite the proximity of Greenholm not very far to
the northeast. The only real life seen here is wildlife (occasionally).
name: Far Downs

desc:
South of the road the terrain becomes quite rough: long grasses blow in the
wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any
form of habitation in the area - the road to the north being the only sign that
anybody ever comes near here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals. To the south the downs appear to be ending, giving way to
bleak, unwelcoming plains. The way west is blocked by a dense grove.
name: Far Downs

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals. To the south the downs appear to be ending, giving way to
bleak, unwelcoming plains.
name: Far Downs

desc:
This cavern is all dark and the ground is covered with a light layer of mud. It
appears to have been abandoned for a very long time, since you cannot see any
tracks on the floor or even the walls. You can hear the sound of running water
coming from somewhere close. Upon looking about carefully, you espy a hole
leading down into the floor of the cavern. The sound of water is louder from the
hole.
name: A Humid Cavern

desc:
The shadows of the trees and the impenetrable ceiling of intertwined branches
makes this place very dark. Even your light almost fails to reveal anything as
the thickness of the grove stops it very quickly. It is hard to advance in any
direction. You think you hear the sound of running water somewhere in the south,
where you can barely see a cave through the dense stand of trees.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
Just grass can be seen in this ground, and its moving when the wind blows is the
only activity you perceive. To the north you see the road, and to the south a
dense and quiet grove blocking your way. There is something disturbing in its
trees.
name: Quiet Fields

desc:
The wind whips across these grassy plains with very little for your protection.
If solitude is what you require then this is the right place to have come. The
only sign of life is a trail, obviously regularly used, amongst the numerous
animal tracks. To the east rise the Far Downs and far to the west, on a clear
day, can be seen the Tower Hills beyond which lies Mithlond. A small pile of
stones has been left here.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The wind seems to blow unimpeded across these green, grassy plains. The Far
Downs rise to the east, and to the south lies a road. Amongst the numerous
animal tracks which criss-cross the land, a well-trodden path can plainly be
seen, leading from the East Road northwards over the plains.
name: Lush Green Grass

desc:
Standing at the edge of the Far Downs with the Grey Havens - Shire Road to the
south and west, a few miles away, and Greenholm a league southeast, the only
sounds to be heard are the blowing of the wind, the rustling of the grass and
the cry of a bird aloft. To the northeast the downs rise in gentle waves and
suddenly stop, the west is dominated by the road running west over a bleak and
desolate plain.
name: Far Downs

desc:
This is the edge of the Far Downs. To the west lies nothing but bleak, open
plains dotted with the odd scrub bush. Far to the west, on a clear day, it is
possible to make out the Tower Hills (S. Emyn Beraid). The ground here rises and
falls quite gently, always climbing to the east though. Animal trails
criss-cross the area, obviously there is a fair amount of wildlife in these
parts, even if there is little in the way of civilisation in evidence.
name: Edge of the Far Downs

desc:
This is the edge of the Far Downs. North and west lies nothing but bleak, open
plains dotted with the odd scrub bush. Far to the west, on a clear day, it is
possible to make out the Tower Hills (S. Emyn Beraid). The ground here rises and
falls quite gently, always climbing to the east though. Animal trails
criss-cross the area, obviously there is a fair amount of wildlife in these
parts, even if there is little in the way of civilisation in evidence. A small
pile of stones has been left here.
name: Edge of the Far Downs

heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals. A small pile of stones has been set here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
