name: Far Downs

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals. To the south, not far away, is a small hamlet.
name: Far Downs

desc:
This small dwelling appears to serve as a stop-over place for the hunters that
frequent the area. A pleasant enough place, it provides shelter from the often
bleak weather of the downs.
name: A Hut

desc:
Once off the road the terrain gets pretty bleak - fine if you like that sort of
thing. The ground here slopes gently upward to the east and south but to the
west the land rears sharply up in the form of a sheer chalk wall... this is the
face of the escarpment that dominates the local landscape.
name: A Dip in the Far Downs

desc:
You are standing in a small, sheltering wood through which a number of small
trails and tracks run. The wind is shut out and all is quiet, really a most
tranquil scene. Through the trees to the west you can make out a small hut in a
clearing. A small pile of stones has been built here.
name: Small Wood

desc:
You are standing on a slight rise in the downs, looking westwards you can
clearly make out the whitish walls of an escarpment face. Below, to the north,
stands a small wood which looks like it offers some shelter from the weather.
Beyond, and all around, the Far Downs roll on for miles on end. You notice a
notch in the scarp far to the south-east.
name: Far Downs

desc:
Once off the road the terrain becomes quite rough: long grasses blow in the
wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any
form of habitation in the area - the road running to the south is the only sign
that anybody ever comes near here. To the west the face of the escarpment gets
larger as you near it.
name: Far Downs

desc:
Once off the road the terrain becomes quite rough: long grasses blow in the
wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any
form of habitation in the area - the road running to the south is the only sign
that anybody ever comes near here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals. A small pile of stones has been set here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
You are standing in a small sheltered hollow amongst the rolling downs. A tiny
stream of water emerges from one side of the hollow trickling down the wall. The
place has a quiet, somewhat serene feel to it - a fine place to rest you might
feel. The place does seem to have an atmosphere of restfulness, it is certainly
oft visited judging from the tracks. A small pile of stones has been set here.
name: A Small Spring

desc:
You stand at the top of a rise, the wind blowing about your body in such a way
that it seems to be driving you away from the vale to the north. All about you
stretch the solemn hills of the Far Downs, bleak yet somehow noble and proud.
All but the vale to the north that is, down there the grass looks pale and not
at all healthy. Not the most pleasant of places it does look like it might offer
some shelter from bad weather.
name: Far Downs

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals.
name: Far Downs

desc:
Once off the road the terrain becomes quite rough: long grasses blow in the
wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any
form of habitation in the area - the road running to the south is the only sign
that anybody ever comes near here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
You are standing among very tall and dark trees that loom above you and seem to
be blocking any sunlight or moonlight from coming through. Strange and wild
animals can be seen moving like shadows in the darkness. You are afraid for your
life and perhaps, the wisest choice would be to make a hasty retreat from this
place towards just about anywhere.
name: Dark Grove

desc:
The grove is dense and dark. The sky is almost totally hidden in the ceiling of
branches, and the silence seems to trap you inside of it. To move in any
direction, you have to open your way and sometimes it gets too hard. To the
south or east, you can try your survival skills. Otherwise, you can return to
the safety of the fields in the north.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
You are standing in a field where the vegetation consists only of the soft,
green grass. To the north you can see the road, and in the south there is a very
dense grove of tall, thick trees. The fields continue to the east and west.
Everything is quiet around here, but frequently you can hear loud and terrifying
noises that come out of the grove. It would be a wise idea to be well prepared
before you enter the grove.
name: Quiet Fields

desc:
Bushes and tall grass are the only signs of life here. The road is to your north
and the fields continue towards east and west. In the south, there is a dense
grove of very tall trees. Just like the fields, the grove is very silent but
occasionally, you can hear loud noises coming from it that suggest the presence
of wild animals inside it.
name: Quiet Fields

desc:
There is silence everywhere. Occasionally, the wind would flow softly making
strange whispering sounds. Otherwise, there appears no danger from any wild
animal. In fact, you notice, there is absolutely no animal in sight from here.
name: Quiet Fields

desc:
This place appears to be just as quiet as the rest of the area. However,
something tells you that sometimes the first looks are not to be trusted. Even
while you feel secure here, you are not sure that everything here is harmless.
name: Quiet Fields

desc:
The grove seems to open here, but just barely. In another time, this must have
been a frequently used path, but somehow it came to be abandoned, perhaps due to
the wild animals that roam the area. The trail is barely wide enough for you to
walk on and continues to the south. The grove gets much denser in the east. All
other exits are blocked by the thickly intertwined branches.
name: A Lost Trail

desc:
Darkness surrounds you and the trees make every move hard. Light barely enters
through the ceiling of branches, and you have a nasty feeling about the whole
place. To the east or south, you could open a way and in the west, you see what
might have been a path in another time perhaps.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
This place is very gloomy and disturbing. The branches of the tall trees are
around you everywhere, blocking every exit. You can try to open your way to the
west, south or east, but perhaps it would be safe to enter the open fields in
the north. The silence and the darkness that is all around you, disturbs you a
great deal.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
The grass fields continue in the west and in the south. Towards north, you can
see a vast area covered with short grass with the ground almost covered with
sharp-edged rocks which might prove fatal if you were to fall upon them. In the
east, you see a very dense grove of tall, dark trees but the network of their
thick and numerous branches prevents entry into the grove. Far in the north, you
think you can see what looks like a wide road.
name: Abandoned Farm

desc:
The fields are covered with tall, wild grass growing on what used to be some
part of a farm. Towards north, the grass is shorter but you see a large number
of jagged rocks in the ground and a stumble might be fatal. The wild grass is
stopped in the west by some huge, ancient looking trees which appear so dense
that any sound coming out of them must be getting muffled before it emerges. In
the east, the grassy fields continue.
name: Abandoned Farm

desc:
You are walking in a very quiet field bordering the south end of the road.
Northwards and eastwards, you can see the road as it bends away from this field.
The grass here is softly moving in the slow wind and that is all the sound that
you can hear. In the south, there is a dense grove of tall trees that prohibits
passage. In the west, you can see very rough fields with jagged stones
half-buried in ground which make it impossible to walk in them.
name: Quiet Field

desc:
The remains of the house are in the same condition as the entire region. There
is nothing here that might remind you of civilisation. The whole place is dark
and infested with rodents. Perhaps, the people who inhabited this area were
driven out by some horribly evil force which still affects the whole region.
name: A Crumbling House

desc:
Under your feet, you notice some signs of what appear to be furrows, implying
that the land was once under cultivation. Towards west, you see the crumbling
remains of what might have been a house standing on a low hill, waiting for the
winds to erode it into complete destruction. In the east, there is a very dense
grove but it's too thick to allow passage from here.
name: Abandoned Farm

desc:
You are walking through a stand of tall grass growing in a disordered way. It Is
hard to advance through it, since your boots find many obstacles hidden in the
grass. You realise that a long time back, this must have been a farm. But now,
the old fields have become covered with wild grass and weeds. Towards northwest,
you can see a decayed wooden structure rising above the ground.
name: Abandoned Farm

desc:
The ground is covered with tall, wild grass although wheat also grows here and
there. Occasionally, you discover a rusted sowing tool half-buried in the top-
soil. A sense of despair weighs all over the place. In the north, there stands a
rotten, old house. It appears that the wild part of nature overpowered the
ordered work of some farming elves.
name: Abandoned Farm

desc:
On the sides of this gloomy trail, the trees grow thick and impenetrable. The
branches are so heavily networked that light barely enters through the roof of
the canopy. There is silence around you, but sometimes a loud, wild noise would
come from far in the east and send shivers up your spine. In the west, you see
an opening in the trees towards what appears to be an old, abandoned field.
name: A Lost Trail

desc:
The dark, tall trees loom over you all around, giving you a nasty feeling that
you should make a hasty exit from this place. You suddenly realise that there is
no other way out but the one you came by. There must be something very evil in
this area. You just cannot avoid thinking that this grove is cursed.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
The path has a gloomy and nasty appearance. The trees surrounding you are more
than decayed - they seem corrupted. A feeling grows in your heart suddenly,
slowly but certainly, that there is some evil force hanging in the air. Its just
a trace, but some time ago, it must have been strong enough to leave a mark now.
To the east, you see a forgotten graveyard, where this feeling seems to be
coming from.
name: A Forgotten Path

desc:
The grove becomes impossible to pass through in the south. However, you can walk
to the north, west and east, although getting the thick branches out of your way
is not an easy task. Everything around you is gloomy and there is a deep
silence. Your instincts tell you to get to a safe place as soon as you can.
name: A Dark Grove

desc:
Here, you cannot tell whether it is day or night. The darkness seems to devour
the light itself. There is something in the black shadows that makes your heart
accelerate and your will to become weaker. The forest itself seems to embody an
ancient evil, perhaps as ancient as the arrival of the firstborns.
name: Black Forest

desc:
The air here is heavy and with a powerful stench. This place has been unknown
for a very long time, which you realise when you look at the ground. All around
you, it is dark and quiet. Beyond the reach of the light you are holding,
something is moving. The shadows are deep and almost look alive. You could get
lost in here or you could lose your mind.
name: Black Forest

desc:
As you walk near the edges of the small bodies of murky water, you can hear soft
rustling sounds. There are plenty of insects in this place. You think you heard
something that sounded like someone moving. Perhaps, it was just your
imagination playing tricks on you. The water here does not look fit for
drinking.
name: Murky Waters

desc:
You appear to have entered a dark grove here. The grove grows very thick in the
east and in the north. To the southwest, you can see grassy plains that seem to
go on forever. Strange and gloomy thoughts arise in your mind as you stay here
and something in your unconscious begins getting stronger. The feeling terrifies
you. Evil stalks the grove ... an ancient evil, whose soul can be felt in each
and every tree of this grove.
name: Dark Grove

desc:
You are standing inside a dense growth of huge, tall and dark trees. The wood of
their trunks and branches looks ancient and almost fragile. But on touching
them, you find that that appearance is deceptive and these trees are as strong
as you can imagine. Exits in every direction are blocked by the branches and
roots and the only obvious exit is to the west.
name: Ancient Woods

desc:
Suddenly, the light from your light source appears to flicker. You sense the
presence of a great evil somewhere close by. You feel some dark feelings in your
unconscious suddenly awaken. You have a tingling feeling all over your body and
a sudden fear grips you. You want to get away as fast as you can. There is a
sign here.
name: Dark Chamber

desc:
The room is wide and circular, and its confines seem covered by a yellowish
mist. The ceiling is a huge dome carrying strange and detailed images in golden
and silver stones, whose meaning could only be found in nightmares. The centre
is a runic circle of powerful, arcane magic, where five pillars burn with green
fire that spreads coldness. The power contained here is so tremendous that you
can almost feel the ground shake under your foot. Your will is fainting, and the
grip on your weapon gets weaker every moment. To the south you see a door with
the most weird runes on its surface.
name: The Chamber

desc:
The air is damp here and does not smell at all good. This is the place where
Nagrorh prays to whatever dark forces control him. It appears that he comes here
very rarely, judging by the dust that has settled every- where. Perhaps he feels
that he is his own lord now.
name: A Dark Temple

desc:
The ambience of this place is mysterious and dark. The remains of old books are
lying scattered around, their forgotten language forever indecipherable. This
place was probably used for private study in solitude, perhaps, of arts and
sciences now long forgotten. As you examine the runes and designs carved on the
walls, you realise that the knowledge of Nagrorh must have been quite
formidable.
name: The Arcane Study

desc:
The iron door slams shut after you. After your eyes adjust to the darkness, you
find that this room is circular and quite spacious. Big pillars hold up a huge
vault ornamented as the blue sky, with a gleaming sun in the middle that glows
very faintly. In the centre of the room, you see an empty fountain surrounded by
plants long dead. You wonder if at another time, this could have been a quite
beautiful place. To the south, you see an arch leading into an obscure chamber.
name: The Underground Garden

desc:
The sound of water dripping reaches you even before your light allows you to see
the inner part of the room. From the ceiling, a constant flow of small drops of
water fall on a puddle that extends from wall to wall. The moisture is so heavy
that you feel it pushing against you. The walls are covered with a thick layer
of moss. From somewhere, a faint sound of rushing water comes and perhaps by
following it, you can reach the surface. The passage to the north is too
slippery to walk.
name: The Humid Room

desc:
The water carries you along downwards a little. When you look around, you see an
underground waterfall which makes it impossible to hear any other noise. The
water flow comes from a wide opening in the ceiling, falling vertically into a
dark hole leading somewhere unknown. Whatever awaits you downstream cannot be
avoided.
name: The Hidden Cascade

desc:
Taking a daring leap, you jumped into the flowing water of this mysterious
underground river. Hitting the rocks, smoothened by the constant grind, is very
painful but tolerable. You are completely uncertain of your final destiny. But
soon you discover yourself at a dark pool where the waterfall ends. You are glad
to be alive.
name: In the Underground River

desc:
As you leap into the hole, you realise it's a slide so steep that is almost a
free fall! Before you can react, the hole over you disappears beyond a curve,
leaving you at the mercy of gravity. Hitting the walls, you advance into the
darkness, expecting the death to appear at any moment to make you suffer the
most ridiculous of the endings. But the slide doesn't lead to a pit filled with
sharp blades. The journey ends with a painful landing, and a bruised ego.
name: Slippery Passage

desc:
You are inside a cavern where an underground river seems to be beginning. From
somewhere in the darkness above, a waterfall comes down, creating a noisy and
turbulent rush. The water reaches your knees here, making your legs and feet
totally wet. From here, the only exit is to the east following the course of the
river.
name: A Waterfall

desc:
The noisy rush of the water fills your ears. Looking down, you see the water
reaches up to your knees. In the east, it enters a narrow tunnel but it is
impossible to go in there. However, you can go west to try and find the source
of the river. In the ceiling, you notice a narrow hole.
name: An Underground River

desc:
Having descended into this well, you feel cut off from the world outside. Any
sound you make is echoed back to you. There are some rocks lying around among
the fungi. The well is dark but some sunlight may still come in. The walls of
this well are made of dry mud. There is absolutely no sign of water anywhere.
But the air is slightly damp and you can sense small vibrations in the ground
under your feet.
name: Deep Dry Well

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals.
name: Far Downs

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
You ascend upon the grassy summit of this small hill. Its crown is covered in
green grass like hair upon its head, but is otherwise empty of any activity.
Slight winds play at your face and you feel the grasses calling out to you to
lie down and sleep with them for awhile and forget your worries. But, you think,
there is much to do before your time is done, so, with a yawn, you wipe away the
spell and look around upon the downs that surround you.
name: Grassy Hilltop

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
Once off the road the terrain becomes quite rough: long grasses blow in the
wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any
form of habitation in the area - the road running to the south is the only sign
that anybody ever comes near here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
You are standing at the ruins of an ancient temple, now partly overgrown. The
roof that once was made of stone lies shattered on the floor and the crumbled
walls are full of holes. The altar at the end of the temple stands rather intact
among the debris. You can tell that not only time has destroyed this once so
beautiful temple but some sort of physical force as well.
name: Temple Ruins

desc:
This pass runs through a valley between the downs. The ground is heavily
littered with loose rocks, with tough, yellowish grass scattered among them.
There is little sound to be heard here, beyond any that might be made by
travellers. Steep hillsides rise up to the east and south, blocking movement in
those directions, while to the west the hills rise more gradually.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
The windswept downs provide a bleak yet austere view. The only sounds to be
heard are the rushing of the wind over the grass and the occasional cry of a
falcon riding the air. Walking here is generally a pleasant and invigourating
experience, provided the weather is good, otherwise it can be an extremely
miserable, disheartening place. Numerous animal tracks criss-cross the area at
regular intervals. To the south the downs appear to be ending, giving way to
bleak, unwelcoming plains.
name: Far Downs

desc:
South of the road the terrain becomes quite rough: long grasses blow in the
wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any
form of habitation in the area - the road to the north being the only sign that
anybody ever comes near here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
Everything is dark and quiet around you, but that does not inspire confidence in
you. You have a terrible feeling about this place. Your hands start to tremble.
The soft slithering noises are echoed again and again by the trees.
name: Black Forest

desc:
A few wooden rocking chairs and a comfortable leather couch surround the
fireplace that blazes during the cold winter months. It looks like many long
nights are spent here, visiting with friends or just pondering until the late
hours. Along the wall is a weapons rack and a place to store armour. An arched
door leads to the bedroom to the east.
name: Gware's Retreat

desc:
This is a small corral, obviously a place the master of this home exercises and
trains his horses. Near the entryway are some flowerbeds full of colourful,
well-tended flowers. Wild rose vines and ivy grow up the wall that surrounds the
corral and the stone cottage. A bench is placed near the backdoor so in the
evening the master and his friends can enjoy the cool night breeze, the scent of
the garden, or even watch the pure bred horses graze.
name: A Horse Corral

desc:
Although this gigantic tree is still alive, the centre was hollowed long ago.
The elegance of this place makes it clear that someone, or something, lives
here. A natural, spiral staircase ascends to a dimly-lit second floor.
name: Wooden Foyer

desc:
You have entered a dark and simple hole which was probably once used as a
storage place. The rotten remains of something can still be found here and
there. Empty crates and barrels are piled up against southern wall.
name: The Cellar

desc:
The single room building is a small but functional home for one of the many
hobbits living in the village. There is a small kitchen area in one corner and a
bedroom area in another corner. However, thin curtain partitions make the two
areas into small rooms in themselves. Right in front of you are a pair of
armchairs and a table and some chairs.
name: Small Hobbit House

desc:
It is quite clear that the owner of this house did not save money in arranging
this room. The massive mahogany desk and chairs are nicely crafted and painted,
on the walls you can see paintings and even the fireplace is well decorated. The
floor is covered by a compact carpet which makes walking comfortable even
without boots. The large window in the western wall gives a nice view over a
well maintained garden.
name: The Cabinet

desc:
You have entered a rather large living room. Most of the space in this room is
occupied by a large square wooden desk placed in the middle. The desk is
skillfully decorated everywhere where it does not hurt its functionality. In the
western wall the huge stone fireplace is placed, which seems to be able to warm
up all the room.
name: The Living Room

desc:
This room has that strange aroma which you can find in all better equipped
kitchens. Along the eastern wall there is a huge and nicely decorated cook-range
with space for a lot of pots. A fine looking cupboard is occupying the western
wall. The cupboard contains a lot of pots, plates and glasses. The other
equipment is having the best position to be able to make cooking fast and
enjoyable as much as possible.
name: The Kitchen

desc:
This is the entrance to a big house built of stone. The accessway from the gate
to the door is made by stone bricks with a small red carpet in front of the
house door. Besides the small accessway someone put a lot of effort to maintain
a garden with many different kinds of flowers. The garden is demarcated to the
north and the south by a small stone wall.
name: The House Entrance

desc:
At the top of the stairs there is another well decorated room. There is a large
four poster bed against one wall, with some heavy red velvet drapes at at the
windows. A large wardrobe leans against another wall.
name: Bedroom in the Greenhands

desc:
You are standing at the bottom of a deep pit. The walls are very smooth and
hard. There is a hole in the roof, but it is beyond your reach. Examining the
walls more carefully, you discover the presence of a tiny stair, carved in the
rock that may allow you to reach higher to a point where there has to be an exit
at the end of the steps. Even though the climbing is rough and quite dangerous,
there is simply no alternative.
name: The Pit

desc:
You find yourself upon a very wide branch, the middle of which has been hollowed
out. The remaining depression is used as a natural basin in which water pools
occasionally, providing an inviting area for birds to bathe in. Several other
branches stretch out to form a kind of platform which is very stable to walk on.
To the west, the branch grows thicker as one makes the way back to the tree
house.
name: A Large Branch

desc:
This hallway is dimly lit by a few torches mounted along the walls. Below your
feet an ornamental carpet rests atop a floor of thick, hardened rock. A strange
bubbling sound seems to come from below. To the west lies a passage of polished
marble leading to a well-lit gallery, while to your east a hallway leads towards
the sleeping quarters.
name: A Dim Hallway

desc:
These quarters have been built to provide a relaxing environment for the warrior
Ganon to rest. There is a large bed in the centre of the room, draped with soft
blankets and pillows of various sizes and colours. An ivory bookcase lined with
many old novels sits alongside the bed. A few torches have been installed over
the entryway to provide soft lighting at night. By day, a large glass skylight
lets the sun into the room.
name: The Sleeping Quarters

desc:
Large torches mounted in metal holders and a roaring fireplace in the centre
light the chamber brightly. Paintings line the north and south walls, flanking a
great mural along the west side of the room. Skilled artists have drawn these
paintings, each of which portrays an epic battle. From time to time, stories of
these battles are told here. Around the room various chairs and tables are
arranged, some of them containing an old book or a table game of some kind.
name: The Entertainment Gallery

desc:
The walls of the chamber are covered with a black-green moss which seems to suck
up all sound and light. The grey stone flagging on the floor is chill under your
feet and is covered by bone fragments. The air currents swirl sluggishly around
you in strange patterns, it feels as if the crypt has a mind of its own. Set in
a niche in the south wall is an ancient coffin with its lid cracked open, it
exudes an evil aura.
name: A Dank Crypt

desc:
This entire area is cluttered with an assortment of books and tomes. At first
glance most of these texts appear to describe spells as they relate to medicinal
knowledge. However, a closer look at the bookcases would also reveal a nice
collection of poetry by many of Arda's famed authors. A desk constructed from
driftwood is located in the right corner of the room, next to an extremely large
bookcase. On the desk a candle burns brightly. The spiral staircase leads up and
down.
name: Nerak's Study

desc:
This small room is dominated by an extremely large bed which is located in the
centre. While the room is sheltered from the rain and cold, several small holes
in the sturdy tree allow you to see the forest and valley below.
name: A Cozy Loft

desc:
Standing at the foot of the escarpment face your passage north is blocked by a
really thick stand of trees and heavy undergrowth. The way west looks like it is
barred by the chalk face, leaving only east and south as routes away from here.
At least there is a modicum of shelter here...
name: At the Foot of the Scarp

desc:
Standing on the edge of the scarp face you can see the downs rolling east
towards the White Hills and further on to the Shire. The gentle dip and climb
speckled by odd pools dotted here and there occasionally reflecting the light.
The view west is of a gentle downslope unfolding into plains as bleak as the Far
Downs and thence to the Emyn Beraid a few leagues further. Unless you can fly it
looks like any easterly travel will be out of the question due to the sheer
drop.
name: Far Downs Scarp Edge

desc:
The place seems to have been unused for many years. Grey tombs rise from the
ground, with the names of long dead persons on their surface. Everything is
ruined and dark, and beyond the grid that surround the graveyard you can only
see decayed trees that seem to escape from it. Just when you were convinced that
the place was completely abandoned, you think to hear some noises from an
impressive grave in the middle of the yard. Your body shivers when you hear the
sounds again...
name: The Abandoned Graveyard

desc:
The air down here has a heavy stench. This place has been closed for a very long
time, as you realise when looking at the dusty ground. On the walls you see the
niches where the occupants of this crypt found a place for their final rest.
Beyond the reach of the light you are holding something is moving. The shadows
are too deep. Maybe this place was not good enough to rest forever.
name: In the Crypt

desc:
The cave widens out into a large chamber. The room is roughly egg-shaped, with
its narrow point near the entrance. Large slabs and chunks of stone have fallen
from the ceiling and cover the floor in broken piles. Water drips from a dozen
cracks in the ceiling, coating every surface of the cavern with moisture and
leaving the floor slick and dangerous. The sharp smell of mildew fills the air.
name: Damp Cavern

desc:
Once off the road the terrain becomes quite rough: long grasses blow in the
wind, the gradual rise and fall of the downs broken by the occasional gully
eroded by the action of the weather on the chalk. There is little sign of any
form of habitation in the area - the road running to the south and a fairly
well-trodden path are the only indications that anybody passes through this way
at all. A small pile of stones has been built here.
name: Far Downs

desc:
Inside these large stables there are many empty stalls for horses and ponies
lining either wall. Each stall has plenty of hay on the floor and a water
trough, as if the innkeeper was expecting lots of travellers to arrive at any
second. At the back of the stables is a large wooden door. There are lots of
muddy horse tracks leading through that door. There is a large note pinned to a
wooden pillar which supports the roof of the stables.
name: Greenholm Stables

desc:
These stairs are made out of stone and can withstand your weight. A thick coat
of dust covers each step suggesting that this place is not known to many people
or that no one has a need for using it. On the walls, you can see a few holders
for lanterns or torches. Some pieces of decayed wood are lying here and there. A
few rusted metal pieces are buried in the dust on the floor.
name: Stairs

desc:
This room is easily double the size of all the other rooms combined. Odors of
prepared foodstuffs tickle the nose as they rise up and out into the open air
above. Several giant stoves are always in use; Baking, stewing, frying and
boiling local favorites as well as delicacies from far away lands.
name: A Large Banquet Hall
